'''Tam Azur-Jamin''' was a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] Human male who served the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] as a [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] and the son of Jedi Knight [[Daye Azur-Jamin]] and [[Tinian I'att Azur-Jamin|Tinian I'att]].

==Biography==
A quiet [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] possibly born on [[Duro/Legends|Duro]], Tam was the [[Padawan/Legends|Jedi apprentice]] of his father. When Daye went missing on [[Nal Hutta/Legends|Nal Hutta]] during the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] sent Tam to find him. Tam returned to [[Eclipse Station]] from Nal Hutta unsuccessful. Tam joined [[Saber Squadron (Eclipse)|Saber Squadron]], flying alongside Luke and [[Mara Jade Skywalker]] under the callsign "Quiet" at the [[Operation Safe Passage|Battle of Talfaglio]]. He later flew against the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] in a special starfighter created on [[Zonama Sekot/Legends|Zonama Sekot]] from a [[seed-partner]] to which he bonded.

Following the end of the Yuuzhan Vong War, Tam wrote the treatise ''[[Droids, Technology and the Force: A Clash of Phenomena]]''. Afterward, Daye Azur-Jamin was discovered on [[Roon/Legends|Roon]], having been turned to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], but was redeemed and reunited with Tam.

During the [[Dark Nest Crisis]], intended to go along with [[Kyp Durron]] to save Luke Skywalker and [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] only to have the mission stopped by [[Corran Horn/Legends|Corran Horn]].

==Behind the scenes==
''[[The New Jedi Order: Star by Star]]'' incorrectly states that he is a [[Duros/Legends|Duros]].

A real world version of [[Droids, Technology and the Force: A Clash of Phenomena (article)|the article]] was written by [[Abel G. Peña]] for [[StarWars.com]]'s [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|Hyperspace]] section. It was published in [[March]] of [[2005]] and attributed to Tam, with Peña credited as the "translator".
